name: Browned-Butter Protein Cookies (Oat, Low-Butter)
description: Lower-butter, oat-boosted spin on the browned-butter choc-chip cookie: butter cut to 8 g, AP flour down to 20 g with 15 g rolled oats folded in, and a splash of almond milk to bring the dough together. To make / test.

payload.text: Don't judge texture off the tray — protein cookies are floppy when hot and feel underbaked. The full 10-15 min rest on the tray is when they firm to chewy.

payload.instructionText: Add dry: stir in the whey, flour, rolled oats, pudding mix, baking soda, and salt until a thick, scoopable dough forms, then fold in the chocolate chips. Add the splash of milk to bring it together so it holds its shape, not pours. Too stiff/dry -> a touch more milk; too loose -> a bit more flour. Let it rest a few minutes so the oats hydrate, then taste for sweetness.

payload.text: Only 8 g of butter — it browns in under 2 min at medium heat. Watch from the moment it melts; the window between nutty and burnt is very short at this small quantity.
Let the dough rest 2-3 min after mixing before adjusting consistency: the oats and whey continue absorbing liquid, and what looks too wet will often firm to scoopable on its own.
9-11 min at 350F — pull while the centers still look underdone and pale. Whey protein bakes through on the hot tray; waiting for a set center means a dry, cakey cookie.
The 30-min chill is load-bearing with only 8 g butter: unchilled dough spreads flat before the edges set, killing the thick-center effect.
